summary: Cleaned up sysdolphin baselib DObj decompilation by replacing large inline-assembly fallbacks for DObjLoad and HSD_DObjLoadDesc with matching C, while preserving one smaller inline-asm fallback for HSD_DObjAlloc because the compiler, referred to in the PR body as Frank, still broke one function or another. The PR also adjusted string symbol declarations/exports and class-info declarations so C could reference existing assembly/data labels and match string placement.
what_changed: Added a Makefile override so $(EPILOGUE_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.o uses CC_EPI := $(CC), matching the existing special-case pattern used for lbtime.o.
Exported asm string labels lbl_80405548 and lbl_80405560 in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.s so C code could reference the existing string data for "sysdolphin_base_library" and "hsd_dobj".
Declared extern HSD_ClassInfo hsdClass in src/sysdolphin/baselib/class.h, replacing the local dobj.c extern that had used HSD_Class.
Changed dobj.c string externs from char pointers to char arrays for labels such as lbl_80405494, lbl_80405548, and lbl_80405560, making the declarations line up with linker labels/string storage.
Removed the NON_MATCHING-gated inline assembly implementation of DObjLoad and enabled the C implementation.
Fixed DObjLoad behavior to match assembly: the 0x40000000 rendermode case now applies flag value 8 with mask 0xE, and the 0x60000000 case applies flag value 4 with mask 0xE.
Changed the DObjLoad error path to call OSReport(lbl_80405494, dobj->mobj->rendermode) and HSD_Panic(lbl_804D5C78, 312, lbl_804D5C80), avoiding literal strings where the original uses data symbols.
Removed the NON_MATCHING-gated inline assembly implementation of HSD_DObjLoadDesc and enabled the C implementation.
Updated HSD_DObjLoadDesc assertion to use lbl_804D5C78 for "dobj.c" rather than a string literal, preserving data-section placement.
Removed local pragma push/peephole/pop around hsdDelete in the shown diff.

Kept HSD_DObjAlloc behind NON_MATCHING/asm, with a comment that the C version really matches but the compiler setup breaks it; the retained asm was slightly cleaned up by changing the default_class load annotation.
Updated the NON_MATCHING C version of HSD_DObjAlloc to use __assert(lbl_804D5C78, 525, lbl_804D5C84).
Updated DObjInfoInit to pass lbl_80405560 for the "hsd_dobj" type string instead of a literal.
smart_moves: Removed the largest inline-asm blocks first and intentionally left the smaller problematic function in asm, minimizing asm surface area while keeping the build/matching state stable.
Converted string literals to existing data labels where section placement affected matching, which directly addressed the earlier comment that functions were non-matching due to string sections.
Used exported asm labels for shared string data rather than duplicating strings in C, preserving original data layout and enabling C functions to match.
Moved hsdClass declaration into class.h as an extern HSD_ClassInfo, making the class-system dependency explicit and reusable instead of keeping an ad hoc local extern in dobj.c.
Corrected the 0x40000000 and 0x60000000 flag mapping based on the deleted assembly, showing careful validation of decompiled switch logic against the original instructions.
Special-cased dobj.o's compiler selection in the Makefile, acknowledging that matching sometimes depends on per-object compiler/toolchain behavior.
decomp_lessons: When a function is non-matching only because of string placement, prefer referencing existing data labels and declaring them accurately as char arrays rather than relying on C string literals.
For linker/asm string labels, extern char label[] is often a better representation than extern char* label because the symbol names the storage, not a pointer variable.
Large inline asm can often be retired by comparing C switch cases directly against branch targets and immediate values in the old asm; this PR found and fixed swapped flag values that way.
If one compiler quirk causes only one remaining function to fail, it can be pragmatic to keep a small asm fallback while converting larger neighboring routines to C.
Per-object Makefile overrides are a valid matching tactic when the default compiler path causes otherwise correct C to break.
Shared class metadata such as hsdClass should live in the relevant header with the correct type so other baselib decompilation units can include it without repeating uncertain externs.
naming_conventions: DObj functions follow the HSD_DObj* public naming style, including HSD_DObjSetCurrent, HSD_DObjLoadDesc, HSD_DObjAlloc, HSD_DObjRemoveAll, and HSD_DObjRemove.
Internal DObj helpers use DObj* names such as DObjLoad, DObjAmnesia, DObjInfoInit, and DObjRelease.
Class-system macros and types use HSD_CLASS_INFO, HSD_PARENT_INFO, HSD_ClassInfo, HSD_DObjInfo, and HSD_DOBJ_METHOD.
Unidentified or data-label symbols retain lbl_-address names such as lbl_80405494, lbl_80405548, lbl_80405560, lbl_804D5C78, lbl_804D5C80, and lbl_804D5C84.
Small data string labels in dobj.c are represented as static char arrays with explicit sizes, e.g. lbl_804D5C78[7] = "dobj.c\0".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed inline asm for DObjLoad and HSD_DObjLoadDesc after making their C versions match.

Kept HSD_DObjAlloc as inline asm under the non-NON_MATCHING path because the PR body says the compiler, Frank, broke either that function or another one; the author chose to leave the smaller function broken/in asm.
Added global labels for existing assembly strings so C references resolve to the same data addresses.
Used label-backed strings in OSReport, HSD_Panic, __assert, and hsdInitClassInfo calls to avoid section/order mismatches from C string literals.
Added a per-object CC_EPI override for dobj.o in the Makefile to influence compiler behavior for matching.
Validated switch behavior from assembly: rendermode masked with 0x60000000 maps 0 to flag 2, 0x40000000 to flag 8, and 0x60000000 to flag 4.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The PR body explains the main tradeoff: most inline asm was removed, but one function had to keep inline asm because the compiler/tooling broke one function or another, and the author chose the smaller function as the remaining problematic case.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that remove the remaining HSD_DObjAlloc inline asm.
Search for other baselib objects using CC_EPI := $(CC) per-object overrides and compare why they need them.
Search for similar string-section fixes using extern char lbl_[] declarations instead of string literals.
Review later class.h changes for hsdClass type consistency across HSD_Class and HSD_ClassInfo.
Find other DObj/MObj/PObj decompilation PRs that depend on rendermode flag mapping.
